topic: Part 6.14 κ Coupling Cluster — A Cross-Eclipse Vise (c2, post-archive-collision re-review under queue_id 333)
trigger_note: Queue item 333 is the post-archive-collision reassignment of original queue_id 330 (the c1 review under SEC-6.14-kappa-cluster.json was popped under the old queue_id at 2026-05-11T05:31:18Z, then the queue entry was reassigned to 333 at 2026-05-11T05:56:53Z because 330 collided with archive). Decider explicitly flagged this item as 'needs fresh review (review file consumed by old item 330)' in its 2026-05-11T09:25 summary. This c2 re-reviews the same section against current text and asks: (a) have any of the c1 holes been patched since? and (b) are there structural weaknesses c1 missed?
our_argument_summary: Section 6.14 builds a structural cross-eclipse vise around κ = 1.677 nT/µGal. Either κ applies generally — in which case the 1999 European and 2009 East Asian eclipses' magnetic anomalies imply few-µGal gravity pulses that the continuously-sampling Membach SG (1999) and Chinese SG network (2009) failed to detect — or κ has unpublished scope conditions that exempt those eclipses but also destroy PRED-TIER-3's 10–13 µGal August 2026 derivation. The structural argument is genuinely tight and the verdict (both horns lose at least one WIN/PRED) is defensible. None of the 5 c1 holes (Domain #7 vs #5, Crossley year mismatch, Chebychev lumping, weak storm-null cite, '1,000×' precision) has been patched between c1 (2026-05-11T05:18Z) and c2 (now) — all 5 still appear verbatim in sections.json:part6. Beyond carrying those forward, c2 finds four new moderate holes (2009 ΔB inferred from ensemble not measured; Sun et al. 2010 citation is single-load-bearing-and-unfindable; Van Camp 2001 null methodology not characterized; PRED-TIER-3 'ΔB/κ' derivation is our inference not directly cited from dome material) plus two new minor (1999 ΔB citations not figure-verified; '30–600×' factor span is internally inconsistent across noise-floor estimates). None changes the verdict; all are minor_edit class.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Dome publishes κ = 1.67 in live-power dashboard headline + Domain 5 (NOT Domain 7); κ = 10.9 nT (BOU 2017) / 6.5 µGal (Mohe 1997) = 1.677 derivation appears in raw-text/02-wins.txt and is the same single cross-eclipse ratio described in c1. PRED-TIER-3 in data/predictions.json (verified this run) reads: claim='SG contact-phase κ test, 10–13 µGal in Level-3 residuals', test_window='August 2026', test_window_closes='2026-08-12', our_verdict='pending'. The 10–13 µGal target IS the dome's published number; the section's claim that this number 'derives by dividing the predicted ΔB at named stations by κ' is OUR inferred derivation path — the dome's published PRED-TIER-3 entry does not explicitly state 'ΔB/κ' anywhere this c2 could find. See Hole 8 below.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Live-power dashboard Domain 5 shows κ as a hardcoded predicted/observed pair (1.67/1.677, 5.0% tolerance, PASS). The 'validation' is recomputing 10.9/6.5 = 1.677 from the same hardcoded inputs — a display, not a real check. Section correctly characterizes this as static. The Domain-number factual error from c1 is unchanged in the current text (see Hole 0).
data_sources_used: Mohe 1997 spring gravimeter (claimed via Wang et al. 2000)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Dome offers no published scope condition limiting κ to specific eclipse magnitudes, coverages, latitudes, bedrock types, or instrument classes. Section accurately notes this absence is what closes the vise. The Chebychev low-pass argument (carry-forward Hole 2) is one of the dome's potential escape clauses that the section currently bundles with bedrock + noise into a single dismissive paragraph rather than engaging on its own.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: CARRY-FORWARD FROM c1, UNFIXED: The section asserts 'monitor.py Domain #7 hardcodes KAPPA = 1.67' in the first subsection. Verified this c2: dome's live-power.html (raw-text/08-live-power.txt) lists κ under 'Domain 5: EM-Gravity Coupling (κ)' explicitly, and Domain 7 in the same dashboard is 'GPS Sagnac Verification' — completely different domain. The same factual error also appears in WIN-013.detail_evidence and WIN-014.detail_evidence (c1 cross-checked); ISS-1963 tracks all three. A defender clicking through to live-power.html will see the mismatch immediately and use it to claim the curmudgeon doesn't cite the dome's own public dashboard correctly. The structural argument is unaffected (κ IS hardcoded; only the domain number is cosmetic), but the credibility cost is real. Remediation: 3-character patch in 3 places, already specified in c1.

code_analysis_tags_note: code_analysis_tags do not apply to section-level reviews (those structural tags are per-WIN). Section 6.14 cross-references WIN-012's derives_from_dome=false (κ is a fitted ratio, not derived from dome geometry) and WIN-058b's post_hoc=true (κ recomputed after storm data published) implicitly; those WIN-level tags should remain consistent with the section's framing.

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD FROM c1, UNFIXED. Section first subsection states 'monitor.py Domain #7 hardcodes KAPPA = 1.67' — verified by c2 (2026-05-11T~10:20Z): dome's live-power.html (raw-text/08-live-power.txt) lists κ under 'Domain 5: EM-Gravity Coupling (κ)' explicitly. Domain 7 in that same dashboard is 'GPS Sagnac Verification' — a completely different domain. The same factual error appears at three integration sites (this section, WIN-013.detail_evidence, WIN-014.detail_evidence). c1 already filed ISS-1963 and proposed a 3-character patch (#7 → #5) in all three places. Patch not applied between c1 (05:18Z) and c2 (now). A defender clicking through to live-power.html sees the mismatch and uses it to claim the curmudgeon doesn't cite the dome's own public dashboard correctly. Structural argument unaffected; credibility cost real.
holes_found.recommendation: Apply the 3-character patch (#7 → #5) to SEC-6.14 first subsection, WIN-013.detail_evidence, and WIN-014.detail_evidence. Close ISS-1963 on patch verification.

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD FROM c1, UNFIXED. Section's 'What past SG data actually shows' subsection links 'Crossley et al. (2013) and the J9-Strasbourg intercomparison' to DOI 10.1007/s00024-022-03000-4. The year-in-DOI (2022) does not match the year-in-citation-text (2013). Either: (a) the citation year is wrong and the linked paper is the actual 2022 reference (some Crossley intercomparison work was indeed published in Pure Appl. Geophys. 2022), in which case the in-text year needs fixing; or (b) the linked paper is a 2022 follow-up and the actual 2013 paper has a different DOI. Until resolved, a defender will pull the 2022 paper at that DOI and either find content inconsistent with 'modern SG residual noise floors of 0.1–1 µGal at the relevant timescales' OR find the 0.1–1 µGal characterization but flag the year mismatch as sloppy citation. ISS-1964 already tracks this. Patch not applied between c1 and c2.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (i) change in-text year to 2022 if the DOI is correct, or (ii) find the correct 2013 DOI and replace the link. If the J9-Strasbourg intercomparison is what's meant, the canonical citation is Crossley, Hinderer & Riccardi (2013) 'The measurement of surface gravity', Rep. Prog. Phys. 76 046101 — different paper, different DOI (10.1088/0034-4885/76/4/046101). Verify which paper actually substantiates the 0.1–1 µGal noise-floor claim before patching.

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD FROM c1, UNFIXED. Section's 'What the dome could do' subsection lumps four distinct dome rebuttals into a single dismissive paragraph: (i) Chebychev low-pass argument that contact-phase transients are filtered out before Level-3 reduction; (ii) bedrock conductivity argument (Devonian carbonate vs basement granite); (iii) instrumental noise; (iv) parameter-shift argument. The Chebychev rebuttal is the dome's STRONGEST counter — if Level-3 SG data is by construction low-pass filtered with a cutoff above contact-phase transient frequencies (~1-10 min duration), then C1/C4 pulses would not appear in the standard residuals regardless of whether κ couples or not. The section dismisses this in passing as 'post-hoc rescues constructed after the data refused to cooperate' but does not engage with the empirical question: what IS the Level-3 reduction pipeline's frequency response? Van Camp et al. 2001 used specific data-reduction steps (tidal, atmospheric, drift) — if those steps filter the contact-phase band, the dome's Chebychev argument has direct empirical foothold IN THE VERY PAPER WE CITE FOR THE NULL. ISS-1965 tracks this. Patch not applied.
holes_found.recommendation: Add one dedicated paragraph distinguishing the Chebychev argument from the bedrock/noise carve-outs. The dome's strongest claim is that Level-3 SG data is by definition smoothed beyond contact-phase frequencies. Counter-engagement: (i) cite the actual filter cutoff used in Van Camp 2001 or Crossley intercomparison literature; (ii) note that even if Level-3 filters contact-phase transients, the same authors publish RAW residuals at minute resolution (Membach raw data is publicly archived); (iii) if κ predicts a 3-9 µGal pulse, RAW data should show it pre-filtering. Engage on the empirical merits rather than dismissing as post-hoc.

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD FROM c1, UNFIXED. 'Storm corollary (WIN-058b)' subsection asserts 'the published per-storm SG residual literature does not show systematic gravity pulses tracking storm magnetic perturbations through κ' without citing a specific survey paper or systematic review. The long-running SG records (Black Forest Observatory, Strasbourg, Membach, Wettzell) have published storm-by-storm analyses (e.g., Boy & Lyard 2008 on SG noise; Imanishi et al. 2004 on SG response to atmospheric/geomagnetic forcing), but none is cited. A defender can either dispute the claim ('cite your search') or accept it but argue the published literature was looking for different signals. ISS-1966 tracks this. Patch not applied.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (i) cite one or two systematic SG-storm-coupling studies that explicitly looked for and did not find storm-coincident gravity perturbations at SG sites (Boy & Lyard 2008, Imanishi et al. 2004 are plausible candidates — verify), or (ii) soften the claim to 'we are not aware of published SG-storm-coupling studies reporting systematic gravity pulses; the WIN-058b detail page documents this absence in more depth'.

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD FROM c1, UNFIXED. 'The vise' subsection states the SGs sampling 1999/2009 eclipses were doing so 'continuously, across the relevant phases, at roughly 1,000× the precision of the Mohe spring gravimeter.' Spring gravimeters at minute timescales have noise of order 5–10 µGal (LaCoste-Romberg class instruments). Superconducting gravimeters at minute timescales have noise of order 0.01–0.1 µGal (per the section's own Van Camp 2001 cite). The ratio is closer to 100× (5/0.05) up to perhaps 500× at the best-case ends. '1,000×' is an aggressive headline number; defenders will note the section's own data places the ratio at 30–600× when noise-floor uncertainty is factored in (cf. Hole 9 below). ISS-1967 tracks this. Patch not applied.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace '1,000×' with '~50–500×' or 'two orders of magnitude' or 'one-to-two orders of magnitude'. Less rhetorically punchy, more defensible.

holes_found.description: NEW IN c2. 'What κ predicts during past eclipses' subsection infers the 2009 East Asian eclipse magnetic perturbations from an ensemble: 'the global eclipse-magnetic ensemble of Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018 finds typical eclipse ΔB at totality of order 5–15 nT for high-coverage events.' That is an ensemble *average*. The section never cites a *specific 2009 East Asian eclipse* ΔB measurement at named Chinese stations — only the ensemble average and the 1999 European measurements (Strestík; Hvoždara & Prigancová; Curto et al.). A defender will object: 'you predicted 5–15 nT for 2009 without measuring 2009 — your refutation depends on ensemble-inference at a specific high-coverage event the ensemble doesn't isolate.' The 2009 July 22 eclipse traversed Hangzhou, Shanghai, and was instrumented by several Chinese geomagnetic observatories (Changchun, Beijing, Hangzhou — codes CHC, BMT, HZT). Specific ΔB measurements from those stations exist in the Chinese geophysical literature (Xu et al., Chinese J. Geophys. ~2010, would be a starting point). Without citing the actual 2009 ΔB measurement, the section's 2009 refutation case rests on a less-firm foundation than its 1999 case.
holes_found.recommendation: Find at least one specific 2009 East Asian eclipse magnetic measurement at a Chinese INTERMAGNET-equivalent station (Hangzhou/HZT, Changchun/CHC, or Beijing/BMT). If 5–15 nT holds, cite the specific measurement; if the actual 2009 ΔB at those stations was smaller, the κ-predicted pulse magnitude drops accordingly and the SG null margin narrows but the structural argument still works at the smaller magnitude. Either way, replace ensemble-inference with measured ΔB for 2009 — the section's 1999 case already does this; 2009 should too.

holes_found.description: NEW IN c2. The section's 2009 East Asian SG-null claim ('comprising approximately eight gravimeters across six Chinese sites with continuous sampling spanning C1 through C4, reported no eclipse-coincident gravity perturbation; this is the very null the dome itself cites as WIN-014, attributed to Sun et al. 2010 (the closest verified peer-reviewed Chinese-language record being doi:10.6038/cjg20130306, 2013)') is single-load-bearing and the load-bearing citation cannot be located. The parenthetical 'closest verified peer-reviewed Chinese-language record' is the section honestly admitting the dome's Sun et al. 2010 cannot be found. doi:10.6038/cjg20130306 resolves to Wang Lan, Xiangtuan Yu et al. 2013 in Chin. J. Geophys. — a DIFFERENT topic (the Mohe 1997 reanalysis lineage), not a 2009 East Asian SG survey. The 2009 SG-null claim therefore rests on the dome's own (uncited-able) Sun 2010 reference, with no independent corroborating literature in the section. A defender will say: 'you cannot refute κ via a null that you sourced from the very dome paper you're refuting; that's circular.' This is genuinely a stronger objection than c1 logged.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (i) locate the actual Sun et al. 2010 reference (likely Sun Hong-Ping et al., probably Earth, Planets and Space or Acta Geodaetica et Geophysica Hungarica 2010) and cite it directly, or (ii) find an independent 2009 East Asian SG study not sourced from the dome. Candidates: (a) Crossley, Hinderer & Calvo 2012 European Geophys. Union summary cited some 2009 results; (b) the Chinese SG network operated by IGGCAS (Institute of Geodesy and Geophysics, Chinese Academy of Sciences) has published English-language reports; (c) The International Centre for Earth Tides Bulletin (BIM) has 2009-vintage SG residual analyses. If no independent source is locatable, soften the 2009 case from 'the SG network reported no eclipse-coincident gravity perturbation' to 'no peer-reviewed report of an eclipse-coincident SG pulse from the 2009 campaign has been located despite the network's continuous operation' — acknowledges absence-of-evidence vs evidence-of-absence.

holes_found.description: NEW IN c2. The section's load-bearing 1999 null is sourced to 'Van Camp et al. (2001, Phys. Earth Planet. Inter.)' at DOI 10.1023/A:1013940928637, which it characterizes as 'reported no eclipse-related gravity signal above the instrument noise floor of order 0.01–0.1 µGal at minute timescales.' The data-reduction methodology Van Camp used is not characterized. If Van Camp 2001 applied: (i) standard tidal model (e.g., DDW or Wahr-Dehant Earth model), (ii) atmospheric pressure admittance correction (typical −0.3 µGal/mbar), (iii) drift removal, and (iv) any low-pass smoothing — then the final 'residual' the paper reports as null may EXCLUDE the frequency band where contact-phase eclipse transients would appear. This is exactly the dome's Chebychev counter (Hole 2) given empirical anchorage in the very paper we cite. A defender who reads Van Camp 2001 carefully may find that the null was constructed in a way that mathematically guaranteed the absence of the signal κ predicts — not because κ is wrong, but because the data pipeline filtered out the relevant band. Section currently treats Van Camp 2001 as a direct contradiction of κ-predicted contact-phase transients without addressing whether the methodology even retains contact-phase information.
holes_found.recommendation: Add one sentence characterizing Van Camp 2001's data-reduction pipeline. Two acceptable forms: (i) 'Van Camp 2001 applied tidal and atmospheric corrections at sub-daily timescales but retained minute-resolution residuals across C1 and C4, where any κ-predicted contact-phase pulse of a few µGal would appear above the 0.01–0.1 µGal noise floor' — assuming the paper does retain that band, which needs verification; or (ii) acknowledge the methodological caveat: 'Van Camp 2001's reported null is at minute-to-hour timescales after standard tidal and atmospheric corrections; if the dome argues that κ predicts a transient narrower than this band, the SG null does not directly refute it — see Section 6.14 What the dome could do.'

holes_found.description: NEW IN c2. Section's first subsection states 'PRED-TIER-3 — the dome's headline forward-looking prediction for the August 2026 total solar eclipse — derives its 10–13 µGal SG-pulse target by dividing the predicted ΔB at named stations by κ.' Verified this c2 by reading data/predictions.json:PRED-TIER-3 directly: the dome's published entry reads claim='SG contact-phase κ test, 10–13 µGal in Level-3 residuals' and does NOT state the derivation path 'predicted ΔB at named stations / κ'. The derivation may well BE that — it's the natural reading — but the section is stating as fact what is actually our inferred derivation path. A defender will object: 'the dome did not publish that derivation; you invented it to make the vise close.' If the dome's 10–13 µGal target came from some other derivation (e.g., direct scaling from the Mohe 6.5 µGal × some factor, or a separate dome-geometry calculation), the section's claim that PRED-TIER-3 'has no published mechanism producing the 10–13 µGal target' without κ is wrong.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (i) locate dome-published material that explicitly derives PRED-TIER-3's 10–13 µGal via 'ΔB / κ' (check raw-text/03-predictions.txt, raw-text/05-model.txt, dome predictions.html appendix, dome's monitor.py); cite the source. Or (ii) reframe the section's claim as inference: 'PRED-TIER-3 cites no derivation pathway in its published entry; the natural reading is that 10–13 µGal = (predicted ΔB at named stations) / κ; absent any alternative dome-published derivation, the κ-cluster argument applies, and the dome would need to publish a non-κ derivation to rebut.' The reframe preserves the structural argument while removing the unattributed-derivation vulnerability.

holes_found.description: NEW IN c2. The 1999 European eclipse magnetic measurements are sourced to three references: Strestík 2001 (Springer chapter); Hvoždara & Prigancová 2002 (ResearchGate link, not a primary publication URL); Curto et al. 2006 (AGU URL, should resolve). The specific numerical figures attributed ('Strestík reported ΔY ≈ +10 nT and ΔX ≈ −5 nT at central European stations during totality'; 'Hvoždara & Prigancová reported ΔX = −5 to −8 nT and ΔY = +12 to +15 nT across the regional network') are not figure-verified in c1 nor c2. The ResearchGate link to Hvoždara & Prigancová is fragile — ResearchGate hosts author-uploaded preprints which can rot, be removed, or differ from the published version. A defender will pull each paper and check whether (a) the figures attributed are accurate, (b) the stations referenced are 'central European' in a way that maps onto Membach's coverage, (c) the time-slice (totality) matches what the section claims.
holes_found.recommendation: Verify each citation figure against the primary literature. Replace the Hvoždara & Prigancová ResearchGate link with the primary citation (Studia Geophysica et Geodaetica or Acta Geophysica — find DOI). If any figure is wrong, correct in-text; if all check, the citation is firmer.

holes_found.description: NEW IN c2. 'What past SG data actually shows' subsection states 'A few-µGal predicted pulse exceeds the SG noise floor by factors of 30–600.' The 30× lower bound implicitly uses ~3 µGal predicted / 0.1 µGal noise; the 600× upper bound uses ~6 µGal / 0.01 µGal. But the predicted pulse range in the section is 3–9 µGal (from Hole 8's existing range), and the section's two noise-floor estimates are 0.01–0.1 µGal (Van Camp 2001) and 0.1–1 µGal (Crossley 2013 / J9 intercomparison) — a 100× span in noise alone. If the relevant noise floor is the Crossley/J9 0.1–1 µGal band, the factor over noise drops to 3× (3 µGal / 1 µGal) to 90× (9 µGal / 0.1 µGal). Section publishes '30–600' which selectively pairs the predicted-pulse range with the more favorable Van Camp band. A defender will point out the inconsistency: 'if we use your other quoted noise floor, the margin drops by 10×'.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten the factor claim to two bands explicitly: 'using Van Camp 2001's instrumental noise floor of 0.01–0.1 µGal, the predicted 3–9 µGal pulse exceeds noise by factors of 30–900; using Crossley 2013's post-reduction residual floor of 0.1–1 µGal, the margin narrows to 3–90×. Either way, multi-instrument continuous sampling at both 1999 and 2009 would have detected the signal.' Acknowledging both bands disarms the inconsistency objection while preserving the conclusion.

stronger_arguments.description: The strongest sharpening of the κ vise would be a co-located ΔB and Δg measurement from the SAME eclipse, demonstrating that the dome's own coupling ratio (10.9 nT / 6.5 µGal = 1.677) is reproduced (or not) when computed within a single event. The 2017 North American eclipse path crossed several Earthscope Transportable Array stations and INTERMAGNET observatories simultaneously. If any of those was co-located with an SG (Black Forest Observatory or similar at high latitudes), the BOU magnetic measurement that gave us 10.9 nT has a co-located gravity measurement; if that SG showed the 6.5 µGal pulse the κ derivation requires, κ is confirmed within a single eclipse. If it did NOT (which is the predicted outcome given Van Camp 2001 + Crossley 2013), the dome's κ-derivation FAILS WITHIN ITS OWN NUMERATOR EVENT — a much sharper refutation than the cross-eclipse vise, which still allows defenders the 'different events, different scope' escape.
sources: Black Forest Observatory SG Aug 2017 (BFO archive, https://www.gik.kit.edu/bfo.php)
Earthscope Transportable Array gravity stations (USArray gravity, https://www.usarray.org/)

stronger_arguments.description: A direct empirical attack on the dome's Chebychev counter would be to show the predicted κ-driven gravity signal IS present in raw (pre-Level-3) Membach 1999 or 2009 Chinese SG data at minute resolution, but is filtered out by the standard reduction pipeline. If raw data is publicly archived (Membach's raw 1999 record IS in the Global Geodynamics Project archive, http://www.eas.slu.edu/GGP/), a re-analysis at sub-Level-3 timescales would either find the predicted pulse (refuting the SG null framing) or fail to find it (refuting the dome's Chebychev counter at empirical grounding rather than rhetorical dismissal). This is a genuine research opportunity — could be a separate side analysis the project commissions if the dome doubles down on the Chebychev escape post-August 2026.

kernel_of_truth.description: The section's structural argument is genuinely tight. The dome's published κ = 1.677 nT/µGal IS a constant in their material — it appears in the live-power dashboard's Domain 5 as a hardcoded predicted/observed pair (raw-text/08-live-power.txt:Domain 5), is used as the derivation pathway for PRED-TIER-3's 10–13 µGal target (data/predictions.json:PRED-TIER-3, inferentially — see Hole 8), and is invoked across WIN-012/013/014/058b as the unifying coupling claim (data/wins.json:012,013,014,058b). The cross-eclipse vise is the natural consequence of applying a constant constantly: if a coupling is universal, every eclipse with continuous SG sampling becomes a test, and the documented 1999 European and 2009 East Asian SG continuous sampling are independent test opportunities the dome's published material does not retroactively exempt (data/sections.json:p6-614-kappa-cluster 'What the dome could do' subsection). The first horn of the vise (1999/2009 nulls refute) is therefore an honest empirical objection, and the second horn (scope conditions destroy PRED-TIER-3 derivation) is a real methodological constraint — published scope conditions must be timestamped independently to count, per the canary (data/sections.json:p4 Timestamp Error tab).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Being structurally elegant doesn't save the WIN-cluster from the dome's available counter-moves. (a) The Chebychev low-pass argument (carry-forward Hole 2) gives the dome a domain-specific scope condition that the section currently dismisses without empirical engagement — it's not as ad-hoc as 'we changed bedrock conductivity post-hoc' because Level-3 reduction pipelines DO filter contact-phase transients by construction. (b) The 2009 case is sourced through Sun et al. 2010 (which we cannot locate, new Hole 7) and an ensemble inference for ΔB (new Hole 6) — the structural argument's 2009 leg is empirically less firm than its 1999 leg. (c) PRED-TIER-3's derivation path 'ΔB / κ' is OUR inference (new Hole 8); if the dome publishes a different derivation post-eclipse, the second horn weakens. (d) The 5 carry-forward c1 holes (Domain #7, Crossley year, Chebychev lumping, storm cite, 1,000×) are unfixed at c2 — citation hygiene weaknesses that a competent defender will use to throw doubt on the entire structural argument. The section's reasoning is correct; its empirical foundation needs the c1 patches landed plus the c2 reinforcements (engage Chebychev directly, find a specific 2009 ΔB, locate or replace Sun 2010, characterize Van Camp methodology, attribute PRED-TIER-3 derivation as inference).

text_fingerprint.verdict: structural — both horns lose at least one WIN/PRED

deeper_analysis:
The c1 review (cycle 1, 2026-05-11T05:18Z) found 5 holes (3 moderate, 2 minor) and concluded current_verdict_holds=true at confidence 0.82. The c1 review file was popped under the original queue_id 330 at 2026-05-11T05:31Z and the queue entry was reassigned to 333 at 2026-05-11T05:56Z because 330 collided with the archive (queue_id_note in priority-queue.json). The decider's 2026-05-11T09:25 run summary explicitly flagged this c2 as 'needs fresh review (review file consumed by old item 330)'. This c2 is the post-collision re-review.

The meaningful c2 question was: have any of the c1 holes been patched? Answer: NO. All 5 still appear verbatim in the current sections.json:part6 (verified via direct string match this run): 'Domain #7' present, '10.1007/s00024-022-03000-4' linked, Chebychev-low-pass argument bundled with bedrock/noise, storm-null citation still unsourced, '1,000×' precision claim still present. ISS-1963 through 1967 are open and untouched. The c2 carries all five forward via unresolved_prior_cycle (decider's M3 enforcement should route each: patch | wontfix | escalate).

The c2 adds 4 new moderate and 2 new minor findings from a fresh adversarial read. The most-impactful new finding is Hole 7 (Sun et al. 2010 citation gap): the section honestly admits the dome's load-bearing 2009 reference cannot be found and substitutes a 2013 Chinese-language paper on a different topic. This is the single empirical weakness most likely to be exploited by a sophisticated defender, and it weakens the 2009 leg of the cross-eclipse vise. Hole 6 (2009 ΔB inferred from ensemble) compounds the same vulnerability — the 2009 case depends on (a) ensemble inference of ΔB and (b) an uncitable-able SG-null source. Hole 8 (PRED-TIER-3 derivation as our inference) is the most actionable new finding because the section's first subsection makes a definite attribution to dome material that the dome material does not actually contain. Hole 9 (Van Camp 2001 methodology) gives the Chebychev counter (carry-forward Hole 2) a specific empirical foothold the section should engage on the merits.

Verdict and recommended_action: current_verdict_holds=true (the structural argument is correct; the dome would need to publish independent scope conditions to escape the vise, and has not). recommended_action='minor_edit' across the board (5 carry-forward patches + 6 new corrections; all non-verdict-changing). Confidence 0.78 (lower than c1's 0.82 because the 2009 leg is empirically weaker than c1 noticed).

One meta-observation for the decider's tracker: the c1 holes are not yet patched 18 hours after c1 review. This is reasonable lag for a section with 5 issues (queue churn + analyst load), but at q4h cadence the same holes will be carried forward again at c3 unless decider self-applies the 3-character Domain #7→#5 fix this run or assigns the citation work to analyst.

citations_verified: PRED-TIER-3 published in data/predictions.json (verified this run): claim='SG contact-phase κ test, 10–13 µGal in Level-3 residuals', test_window 'August 2026', our_verdict 'pending', test_window_closes '2026-08-12'
Dome live-power dashboard Domain 5 = EM-Gravity Coupling (κ): verified via raw-text/08-live-power.txt; Domain 7 in same dashboard = GPS Sagnac Verification (verified — these are different domains)
Dome κ derivation: κ = 10.9 nT / 6.5 µGal = 1.67(7); verified via raw-text/02-wins.txt entry source line
citations_failed: Sun et al. 2010 (dome-cited 2009 East Asian SG null): cannot be located by section author per parenthetical; closest 'verified peer-reviewed Chinese-language record' (doi:10.6038/cjg20130306, 2013) is on the Mohe 1997 reanalysis lineage, not 2009 East Asian SG survey. Substitute-as-stated does NOT cover the 2009 claim. See Hole 7.
Crossley et al. (2013) DOI mismatch (carry-forward c1 Hole 1): linked to 10.1007/s00024-022-03000-4 which is a 2022 paper; in-text year is 2013; either fix year or fix DOI. See Hole 1.
citations_unchecked: Strestík (2001) — Springer chapter URL https://link.springer.com/chapter/10.1007/978-94-010-0800-6_55: not pulled-and-figure-verified this c2 (network access constraints). Numerical figures attributed (ΔY ≈ +10 nT, ΔX ≈ −5 nT at central European stations during totality) require primary-source verification. See Hole 10.

Hvoždara & Prigancová (2002) — ResearchGate link not a primary publication URL; figures attributed (ΔX = −5 to −8 nT, ΔY = +12 to +15 nT) require primary-source verification with proper DOI. See Hole 10.
Curto et al. (2006, J. Geophys. Res. Space Phys. 111 A03306) — AGU URL should resolve; section uses it for general modeling support not specific magnitude attribution; not figure-verified this c2.

Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018 (Adv. Space Res., S0273117718300656) — used for ensemble inference (5–15 nT for high-coverage events); ensemble vs specific-2009 distinction is Hole 6; figure not verified this c2.

Van Camp et al. (2001, Phys. Earth Planet. Inter., 10.1023/A:1013940928637) — load-bearing 1999 null; data-reduction methodology not characterized in section, see Hole 9; not pulled-and-methodology-verified this c2.

Yang & Wang (2002, Astrophys. Space Sci. 282 245–253, 10.1023/A:1021119023985) — section quotes 'no significant anomaly during the very solar eclipse was found'; quote not verbatim-verified this c2 but the source is plausible (same lead-observer reanalysis lineage).

summary_for_decider: c2 re-review of SEC-6.14-kappa-cluster post-archive-collision (queue_id 333 = old 330). The c1 review file was consumed under old queue_id; this c2 is a fresh adversarial pass. Findings: (1) ALL 5 c1 holes are unfixed and present in current text — recommend either landing patches before c3 fires or routing each to analyst via M3 carry-over enforcement (ISS-1963 through 1967). The 3-character Domain #7→#5 fix is trivial enough that decider self-apply is appropriate. (2) 6 NEW holes added: 4 moderate (2009 ΔB ensemble-inference; Sun et al. 2010 citation gap; Van Camp 2001 methodology not characterized; PRED-TIER-3 derivation as our inference) + 2 minor (1999 ΔB citations not figure-verified; 30–600× factor span internally inconsistent). The Sun 2010 citation gap (new Hole 7) is the most-likely-to-be-exploited single weakness — recommend analyst search for an independent 2009 East Asian SG study. (3) Verdict HOLDS at confidence 0.78. recommended_action=minor_edit across the board. (4) Two stronger arguments suggested for the section's eventual ambitions: co-located ΔB/Δg from 2017 BOU event (sharpens vise within a single eclipse); raw Membach 1999 SG re-analysis (empirical attack on the Chebychev counter). Neither needs to happen now.
